articles.title: Anthropic 探索三星 2 纳米自研芯片：绕开英伟达路径迈出实质一步
articles.title_en: Anthropic Explores Samsung 2nm Custom Silicon: A Substantive Step Bypassing Nvidia
articles.summary: 据 The Information 2026 年 7 月 2 日报道，Anthropic 正与三星洽谈基于其 SF2（2 纳米）工艺的自研 AI 推理芯片合作，双方已进入设计验证阶段。若合作最终落地，首批芯片预计 2027 年下半年流片、2028 年初小规模量产。Anthropic 的自研目标是「推理专用 SoC」——针对 Claude 系列模型的推理特点做深度定制，重点优化 KV Cache 带宽、注意力机制内存访问、长上下文推理三个环节。这是 Anthropic 首次采取实质动作绕开英伟达的 GPU 供应链——若成功，可能标志 AI 头部厂商全面走向自研芯片时代。
articles.summary_en: According to The Information on July 2, 2026, Anthropic is in advanced talks with Samsung on a custom AI inference chip built on Samsung's SF2 (2nm) process, having entered design verification. If completed, first tapeout is expected in H2 2027 with limited production in early 2028. Anthropic's goal: an inference-specific SoC deeply customized for Claude's inference profile, with emphasis on KV cache bandwidth, attention memory access, and long-context inference. This is Anthropic's first substantive move to bypass Nvidia's GPU supply chain — if successful, it could mark a broader shift toward in-house silicon across frontier AI vendors.
articles.source: The Information 2026-07-02 / Bloomberg / 电子工程专辑 2026-07-03 / EE Times China
articles.source_en: The Information 2026-07-02 / Bloomberg / EE Times China 2026-07-03
articles.content: Anthropic 自研芯片的战略背景有三条：其一，Fable 5 剥离方案的高单价（$10/$50 每百万 Token）核心成本压力仍来自英伟达 GPU 供应；其二，Google TPU、Meta MTIA、微软 Maia、AWS Trainium 等自研芯片路径已相继落地，Anthropic 是最后一个头部 AI 厂商没有自研方案的公司；其三，三星 SF2 相比台积电 N2 的价格更具竞争力，且三星愿意接受深度定制。三星方面提供的合作条件据传是「独家 SF2 产能优先 + Foundry+DRAM 一体化供应」——这份组合是台积电目前无法匹配的。业内评估：Anthropic 若走通，Claude 推理成本可能从当前每百万 Token 的 $4-8 边际成本下降到 $2-4——相当于把 Fable 5 定价的核心成本项优化 50%+。这可能是 2027 下半年 Fable 6 或 Claude 后继模型再度打破商业化格局的关键变量。
articles.content_en: Three drivers behind Anthropic's silicon push: (1) Fable 5's high list price ($10/$50 per million tokens) is core-cost-anchored to Nvidia GPU supply; (2) Google TPU, Meta MTIA, Microsoft Maia, and AWS Trainium have all shipped in-house silicon — Anthropic is the last frontier lab without one; (3) Samsung SF2 is more price-competitive than TSMC N2, and Samsung is willing to accept deep customization. Samsung's reported package: "exclusive SF2 capacity priority + integrated foundry + DRAM supply" — a combo TSMC currently cannot match. Industry estimate: if Anthropic pulls it off, Claude's per-million-token marginal inference cost could drop from today's $4-8 to $2-4 — a 50%+ optimization of Fable 5's core cost line. This could be the key variable for Fable 6 or Claude's next-gen model to reshape commercialization again in H2 2027.

首次绕开英伟达 GPU 供应链的实质动作
预估 Claude 推理边际成本可降 50%+，为 Fable 6 铺路
key_points_en: Anthropic × Samsung SF2 (2nm) custom AI inference chip talks
Design verification underway; tapeout H2 2027, production early 2028
Inference-specific SoC optimizing KV cache / attention / long context
First substantive step to bypass Nvidia's GPU supply chain

articles.title: 具身智能商业化落地加速：星动纪元/傅利叶智能双双获批国内首批人形机器人「劳务外派」订单
articles.title_en: Embodied AI Commercialization Accelerates: Robot Era and Fourier Both Win China's First Humanoid "Labor Dispatch" Contracts
articles.summary: 2026 年 7 月 3 日，具身智能公司星动纪元与傅利叶智能双双宣布，其人形机器人 XR-2 与 GR-2 已获批国内首批「机器人劳务外派」订单——星动纪元与上海某汽车零部件厂签订 200 台机器人 3 年劳务合同、总金额 1.2 亿元人民币；傅利叶智能与东莞某电子代工厂签订 320 台机器人 5 年合同、总金额 2.4 亿元。两份合同都采用「按机器人可用工时付费」的新模式——工厂不购买机器人本体，而是按每小时可用工时计费，每小时约 25-40 元人民币，接近当地初级劳动力的 60-70%。这是中国人形机器人从「展示级」正式走向「产线级」的第一份规模化商业闭环订单。
articles.summary_en: On July 3, 2026, embodied AI companies Robot Era and Fourier Intelligence both announced China's first "robot labor dispatch" contracts for their humanoid robots XR-2 and GR-2. Robot Era signed a 200-unit, 3-year labor contract worth 120M RMB with an auto parts factory in Shanghai; Fourier signed a 320-unit, 5-year contract worth 240M RMB with a Dongguan electronics contractor. Both contracts use a novel "pay-per-robot-available-hour" model — the factories don't buy the robots, but pay ~25-40 RMB per available robot-hour, roughly 60-70% of local entry-level labor cost. This marks the first commercial-loop scale contracts moving China's humanoid robots from "demo tier" to "production line tier."
articles.source_en: 36Kr 2026-07-03 / Zhidx 2026-07-03 / The Paper / Robot Era Newsroom
articles.content: 两份合同的关键点在于「劳务外派」而非「机器人销售」——这是国内首次由制造业厂商与机器人公司直接以劳动力计价单位签约。合同结构：机器人公司提供机器人本体、软件更新、故障响应、能源+运维保障；工厂提供工位、电力、通讯网络，按可用工时（每台机器人每天可承接 12-16 小时任务）付费。合同风险由机器人公司承担——机器人故障期间不计费。这份定价结构相当接近传统「派遣工」劳务模式——但劳务方是机器人公司而非人力资源公司。星动纪元合同覆盖场景：汽车零部件的搬运、检测、简单组装、AOI 缺陷筛选；傅利叶合同覆盖场景：电子产品的贴片检测、外观品检、包装分拣。两份合同都对「可用工时 KPI」有明确约定——低于 85% 可用工时的部分需赔付。这标志中国人形机器人从「概念验证」正式进入「按小时收费」的规模化商用阶段，也可能是全球第一份此类规模化「机器人劳务外派」合同。
articles.content_en: The critical detail is "labor dispatch" rather than "robot sales" — the first time Chinese manufacturers and robot companies have contracted directly in labor-hour units. Structure: the robot company supplies the hardware, software updates, fault response, energy + maintenance; the factory supplies workstation, power, network, and pays per available hour (each robot can take 12-16 hours of tasks per day). Downtime risk sits with the robot company — no billing during faults. The pricing structure closely mirrors traditional "dispatched labor" contracts, except the labor supplier is a robot company. Robot Era's scope covers auto parts handling, inspection, simple assembly, and AOI defect screening; Fourier covers SMT inspection, appearance QC, and packaging sortation. Both contracts include explicit "available-hour KPI" clauses — below 85% availability triggers penalty payments. This marks China's humanoid robots moving from PoC into an hourly-billed commercialization phase, and may be the world's first scaled "robot labor dispatch" contracts.
key_points: 星动纪元 200 台 3 年 1.2 亿元、傅利叶 320 台 5 年 2.4 亿元
「按机器人可用工时付费」模式：25-40 元人民币/小时
接近当地初级劳动力成本的 60-70%

国内人形机器人从「展示级」进入「产线级」商业闭环

"Pay-per-robot-available-hour": 25-40 RMB per hour

hero.title: 全球 AI 三巨头开闸日：GPT-5.6 全网开放、Grok 4.5 面向公众、DeepSeek 与智谱双双秘密造芯
hero.title_en: Global AI Floodgates Day: GPT-5.6 Goes Worldwide, Grok 4.5 Opens to Public, DeepSeek & Zhipu Both Secretly Building Chips
hero.summary: 2026 年 7 月 9 日是全球 AI 产业分水岭：一边是 OpenAI GPT-5.6 完成商务部 CAISI 批复正式全球开放 Sol / Terra / Luna 三档、Grok 4.5 面向公众开闸并与 Cursor 联合训练成为首个编程 Agent 专用模型，另一边是路透社与 The Information 同日曝出 DeepSeek 秘密启动推理芯片项目一年、智谱 GLM-5.2 上线首周日 token +27 倍触发自研 ASIC——同一天英伟达 5/14 高点回撤 16%、市值蒸发约 1 万亿美元、前瞻 PE 跌至 18 倍七年新低。CNBC 与 OpenRouter 同期披露：中国开源模型在美国 API 市场周占比自 2/8 起稳定 30% 以上、峰值触 46%，Coinbase、Airbnb、Lindy 集体切换。软件端「去英伟达化」与硬件端「去英伟达化」同时启动，全球 AI 算力生态迎来结构性裂变。
hero.summary_en: July 9, 2026 marks a watershed for global AI: on one side, OpenAI GPT-5.6 clears CAISI review to go worldwide across Sol / Terra / Luna tiers while Grok 4.5 opens to the public and, co-trained with Cursor, becomes the first coding-agent-focused frontier model; on the other side, Reuters and The Information simultaneously reveal that DeepSeek has been quietly building an inference chip for a year and Zhipu—after GLM-5.2 saw a 27x daily token surge in its first week on Vercel—has started its own ASIC. On the same day Nvidia is down 16% from its May 14 peak, roughly USD 1 trillion in market cap gone and its forward PE at a seven-year low of 18. CNBC and OpenRouter report that Chinese open models have held a 30%+ share of the US API market since Feb 8, peaking at 46%—with Coinbase, Airbnb, and Lindy all switching. Software-side and hardware-side «de-Nvidiafication» begin on the same day, and the global AI compute ecosystem enters a structural fracture.
